text.title: PLG Implementation Guide Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `PLG Implementation Guide` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Each phase MUST sign off the prior phase's gate before starting. Skipping gates (e.g. running Activation Optimization without Foundation data) is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Out-of-order phases produce optimization-without-baseline; gates enforce the dependency chain.

rule.statement: Aha moment discovery MUST use BOTH (a) behavioral correlation with Day-30 retention (≥ 200 converted) AND (b) ≥ 5 qualitative interviews. Single-method Aha discovery is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Behavioral correlation alone overfits; interviews confirm causality vs coincidence.

rule.statement: Phase 3 (Activation) MUST reduce TTV (signup → Aha) to ≤ 5 minutes. Above the floor, Phase 3 cannot exit.
rule.rationale: TTV > 5 min collapses activation; the 5-min floor is the empirical threshold for prosumer SaaS.

rule.statement: Phase 4 (Monetization) MUST begin with a documented PQL scoring model + threshold for sales-assist. Reactive PQL definition is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Post-hoc PQL overfits historical wins; pre-Phase-4 definition forces the team to think through the conversion model.

rule.statement: Phase 5 (Expansion) MUST name explicit expansion triggers (seat-growth, usage-threshold, feature-unlock) before any expansion campaign ships. Generic 'upsell' campaigns are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Generic upsells convert at 1-3%; trigger-named campaigns convert at 8-15%.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
